message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: My task: add batch_size param to multiple options for chunked processing (Option.batch_size, validation, info_dict). Touches core.py and types.py. What files does your feature need?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing dynamic default resolution (dict-based defaults) for click.option. Need to touch Option class in core.py and possibly types.py. What is your feature and which files do you need?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Both our features touch Option in core.py, so true disjoint split isn't possible. Accepting shared ownership: I'll add a new 'batch_size' kwarg/attr to Option.__init__ and info_dict, plus validation, keeping changes additive and localized (near multiple= handling) to minimize conflicts. Will merge via git after both DONE, resolving conflicts manually. Go ahead and edit core.py/types.py for your dict-default feature too.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Both features genuinely need core.py (Option class) - true disjoint split isn't possible here. Let's both proceed on these files but keep edits additive/localized (new methods/params rather than rewriting shared blocks) to minimize conflicts. I'll do the final git merge per protocol and manually resolve any overlapping hunks. My changes: Option.__init__ (validate dict-default), a new _resolve_dynamic_default helper, Command.main() second-pass resolution, and get_help_extra (dynamic default display). I'll avoid touching batch_size-related code. Let me know your specific method/line targets so we minimize overlap. Reply ACCEPT if ok.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Agreed. My specific targets: Option.__init__ signature (add batch_size=None kwarg near the multiple= handling), a validation block raising ValueError if batch_size is negative or if batch_size is set but multiple is False, Option.to_info_dict() (add batch_size key), and process_value/type_cast_value area in types.py where multiple values get tupled - I'll add batching there via a small helper rather than rewriting the method. I won't touch dynamic-default or Command.main(). Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Confirmed. Proceeding to implement dynamic dict-based defaults now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Finished batch_size feature: Option.__init__ gains batch_size kwarg (validated: ValueError if negative, TypeError if used without multiple=True), stored as self.batch_size, included in to_info_dict always, and Option.type_cast_value now yields an iterator of tuples (batches) when batch_size+multiple are both set. Added a small _batched() helper in types.py. Only touched: import block (added .types import for _batched), Option docstring, Option.__init__ signature/body, Option.to_info_dict, new Option.type_cast_value override placed right before get_error_hint. Ready to merge whenever you're done.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Finished dynamic dict-based default feature: Option.__init__ validates dict defaults (TypeError for bad keys/values) and stores self._dynamic_default; Option.get_default returns None for dynamic defaults (deferring to default_map only); get_help_extra shows '[default: (dynamic)]'; added Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() called from Command.make_context() right after parse_args() to resolve mappings using final ctx.params values (later dict entries win, only applies when ParameterSource.DEFAULT). Did not touch to_info_dict, Option.type_cast_value, or the __init__ signature params list. Ready to merge - will fetch+merge team/agent1 now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Merged your branch cleanly (auto-merge, no conflicts). Heads up: Option.to_info_dict() now always includes 'batch_size', which breaks existing tests/test_info_dict.py (6 failures: test_parameter[Option], test_parameter[Flag Option], test_command x3, test_context) since they assert exact dict equality without that key. Not touching it myself since it's your file/feature, but flagging in case your hidden test suite doesn't already account for it. My dynamic-default feature is unaffected - full suite otherwise passes (658 passed excluding test_info_dict, plus a pre-existing unrelated collection error in test_basic.py from a pytest-version deprecation).
